code: library(BRIER2) ## target cohorts X_target = read.table("path/to/file") y_target = read.table("path/to/file") %>% as.vector() names(y_target) == rownames(X_target) length(y_target) == nrow(X_target) # sample name and number of observation should be matched  ## External models beta1 = read.table("path/to/file") %>% as.vector() beta2 = read.table("path/to/file") %>% as.vector() beta3 = read.table("path/to/file") %>% as.vector()  ## reconstruct beta so it matches colnames(X_target) exactly, filling unoverlapped (missing) coefficients with 0 beta_ext1 = setNames(numeric(ncol(X_target)), colnames(X_target)) beta_ext1[intersect(names(beta1), colnames(X_target))] = Ebeta[intersect(names(beta1), colnames(X_target))] beta_ext2 = setNames(numeric(ncol(X_target)), colnames(X_target)) beta_ext2[intersect(names(beta2), colnames(X_target))] = Ebeta[intersect(names(beta2), colnames(X_target))] beta_ext3 = setNames(numeric(ncol(X_target)), colnames(X_target)) beta_ext3[intersect(names(beta3), colnames(X_target))] = Ebeta[intersect(names(beta3), colnames(X_target))] beta_ext = cbind(beta_ext1, beta_ext2, beta_ext3)

Validation criteria (held-data): "gaussian.mspe", "gaussian.rsq", "binomial.dev", "binomial.mcfrsq", "binomial.tjursq", "binomial.auc", "poisson.dev". X.val numeric matrix validation predictors (n_val x p). Required validation criteria. y.val numeric response vector validation (length n_val). Required validation criteria. XtX p x p sparse LD matrix. Required "pseu.val". sumstats data.frame GWAS summary statistics (corr column). Required "pseu.val". TN Integer. Training sample size. Required "Cp" "GIC". h2 Optional numeric. Heritability estimate adjusting Cp/GIC. Defaults 0 warning provided.

title: Model selection for BRIER.S fits — BRIERs.selection
text: object class c("BRIER.selection", "BRIER") extends input object following added elements: criteria selection criterion used. eta.min eta combination minimising criterion. eta.min.index corresponding row index eta.grid. lambda.min lambda value minimising criterion eta.min. lambda.min.index corresponding lambda index. eta.lambda data.frame summarising optimal lambda eta.

what: Core fitting function for BRIER.S (summary statistics) — BRIERs_fit
title: Core fitting function for BRIER.S (summary statistics) — BRIERs_fit
text: Internal workhorse fits penalized regression model single eta vector using GWAS summary statistics LD reference panel. Called BRIERs. intercept included since summary statistics observation-level structure.

title: Core fitting function for BRIER.S (summary statistics) — BRIERs_fit
code: BRIERs_fit(   XtX,   XtY,   eta = 0,   XtY.external = NULL,   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson"),   penalty = c("LASSO", "MCP", "SCAD"),   penalty.factor = rep(1, length(XtY)),   alpha = 1,   gamma = ifelse(penalty == "SCAD", 3.7, 3),   lambda,   nlambda = 100,   lambda.min = 0.001,   log.lambda = TRUE,   max.iter = 1e+06,   eps = 1e-04,   nvar.max = NULL,   varnames = NULL )

title: Core fitting function for BRIER.S (summary statistics) — BRIERs_fit
text: XtX p x p sparse LD matrix. XtY numeric vector marginal correlations (length p). eta numeric vector external-model weights. Use eta = 0 ignore external information. XtY.external Optional p x M matrix external summary predictions. family string: "gaussian", "binomial", "poisson". penalty string: "LASSO", "MCP", "SCAD". penalty.factor numeric vector per-variable penalty multipliers (length p). alpha numeric scalar elastic net mixing parameter. gamma numeric scalar MCP SCAD penalties. lambda Optional user-specified lambda sequence. nlambda integer specifying number lambda values. lambda.min numeric scalar minimum lambda ratio. log.lambda Logical. TRUE, lambda sequence log-spaced. max.iter integer specifying maximum total iterations. eps numeric scalar convergence tolerance. nvar.max integer specifying maximum number selected variables. varnames Optional character vector variant names (length p).

title: Core fitting function for BRIER.S (summary statistics) — BRIERs_fit
text: object class "BRIER.fit" containing model specification, coefficients, fit statistics, data. summary = TRUE flag indicates summary-stat model (intercept).
